commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A repair worker can address the concrete completion-correlation, docs, storage, and hot-path defects; maintainers still need to decide whether the broader bundled Webhooks security/config surface should land.; Needs attention: The diff is security-sensitive because completion delivery can expose wrong run output, dynamic subscriptions persist generated webhook secrets, and exec delivery runs configured local... (sha=70e8bacdb5af5203ca249230958d1c1ead63308b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR expands the bundled Webhooks plugin with generic authenticated HTTP ingress, dynamic Gateway-managed subscriptions, CLI commands, dispatch/delivery/completion paths, and enterprise docs.

PR surface: Source +3563, Tests +2926, Docs +1401. Total +7890 across 34 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection on the PR head shows completion context is registered before schedule acceptance, Gmail CLI docs diverge from still-registered Gmail commands, and subscription loading still uses a JSON file on the ingress path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A repair worker can address the concrete completion-correlation, docs, storage, and hot-path defects; maintainers still need to decide whether the broader bundled Webhooks security/config surface should land.

target.title: Add generic webhooks plugin
target.body:
## Summary

- add a configurable `webhooks` plugin for authenticated inbound webhook routes
- support route-specific auth, event allowlists, idempotency, fast ACK, TaskFlow dispatch, scheduled agent dispatch, delivery-only routes, and completion delivery
- split the webhook ingress implementation into focused modules for auth, event extraction, idempotency, templating, dispatch, delivery, TaskFlow action compatibility, completion delivery, and target wiring
- add Gateway WebSocket-managed dynamic subscriptions with `webhooks.subscribe`, `webhooks.list`, `webhooks.remove`, and `webhooks.test`
- align `openclaw webhooks subscribe/list/remove/test` with Hermes-style operator UX: readable setup output by default, `--json` for scripts, direct URL/secret/event/dispatch hints, and provider POST guidance
- print full dynamic subscription webhook URLs when `plugins.entries.webhooks.config.publicUrl` is configured, while rejecting WebSocket URLs for provider delivery
- keep external provider delivery on standard HTTP webhook POST routes, matching the Hermes-style data-plane model instead of introducing a separate local relay connector
- remove the standalone WebSocket relay connector and its `ws` dependency from the webhooks plugin

## Verification

- `PATH=/Users/bytedance/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:$PATH node scripts/test-extension.mjs webhooks`
  - 4 files passed, 55 tests passed
- `PATH=/Users/bytedance/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:$PATH pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project extensions/webhooks/tsconfig.json`
- `git diff --check`
- attempted full-repo `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project tsconfig.json --incremental false`
  - failed from TypeScript/Node heap OOM at both default heap and `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`; no TypeScript diagnostics were produced before OOM

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Enterprise systems can post authenticated webhook events into OpenClaw and trigger TaskFlows, scheduled agent runs, or delivery actions without hard-coding a specific provider. Dynamic route management goes through OpenClaw Gateway RPC, so CLI/operator control uses the existing Gateway WebSocket plane. External providers still use standard HTTPS webhook POST routes for the data plane.

Fresh Codebase MR E2E, current PR build:

- System: Codebase
- Event: `merge_request`
- routeId: `codebase_mr_review`
- Webhook URL: `https://annelle-figured-intimately.ngrok-free.dev/plugins/webhooks/codebase-mr-review` (temporary ngrok tunnel for development proof only)
- Real object URL: `https://code.byted.org/iaasng/openclaw-session-search/merge_requests/9`
- Real trigger: pushed commit `21a7c63` to MR branch `codex/openclaw-webhook-e2e-20260528105608`
- Delivery ID: `776844360074903`
- OpenClaw response: `202 Accepted`
- Scheduler proof: cron job `f6c33620-92ea-4dcd-9af2-5365a5d0f6db` added, followed by `[webhooks] agent dispatch scheduled`
- Agent task/run: task `889ffffa-fcc5-4903-bf81-6a32d219d4d5`, run `cron:f6c33620-92ea-4dcd-9af2-5365a5d0f6db:1779957859480`, status `succeeded`
- Agent actual action: reviewed real MR `!9` and produced an approved review summary for the README-only validation change
- Writeback result: Codebase review note `776844435579774` created at `2026-05-28T08:44:53Z`
- Completion proof: logs show `[webhooks] exec delivery completed` and `[webhooks] delivered agent completion`
- Duplicate replay: not rerun for this fresh delivery; historical duplicate/idempotency coverage remains in targeted tests
- Conclusion: passed on current PR build `c3fce7e`

Fresh GitHub PR E2E, current PR build:

- System: GitHub
- Event: `pull_request`
- routeId: `github_pr_review`
- Webhook URL: `https://annelle-figured-intimately.ngrok-free.dev/plugins/webhooks/github-pr-review` (temporary ngrok tunnel for development proof only)
- Real object URL: `https://github.com/marchpure/openclaw-webhook-e2e/pull/1`
- Real trigger: created PR `marchpure/openclaw-webhook-e2e#1` from branch `openclaw-webhook-e2e-20260528165421`
- GitHub hook id: `632275130`
- Delivery ID: `d644f110-5a72-11f1-8ca6-4ff9e4ff95e1`
- OpenClaw response: `202 Accepted`
- Agent task/run: task `268f04a3-996e-4bde-b81e-c6455b984606`, run `cron:39c4bcf0-1df9-4dc5-a01d-cfcf7934c592:1779958472520`, status `succeeded`
- Agent actual action: fetched/reviewed the real PR diff and approved the README marker change
- Writeback result: PR comment `IC_kwDOSqKJKM8AAAABD--Qrg`, URL `https://github.com/marchpure/openclaw-webhook-e2e/pull/1#issuecomment-4562325678`
- Completion proof: PR comment appeared at `2026-05-28T08:55:30Z`
- Duplicate replay: not rerun for this fresh delivery; idempotency behavior remains covered by targeted tests
- Conclusion: passed on current PR build `c3fce7e`

Gateway WebSocket and Hermes UX proof:

- `webhooks.subscribe`, `webhooks.list`, `webhooks.test`, and `webhooks.remove` are registered through the Gateway RPC surface and exposed under the built-in `openclaw webhooks` CLI parent.
- The CLI now mirrors Hermes webhook UX: `subscribe` prints URL, secret, events, dispatch, prompt preview, and the instruction to configure the source service to `POST` to that URL; `list/remove/test` print readable operator output; every command keeps `--json` for automation.
- Dynamic subscriptions return a full `webhookUrl` when the Webhooks plugin has an HTTP(S) `publicUrl`; `wss://` URLs are rejected because provider delivery is HTTP, not WebSocket.
- Local proof used `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:18889` plus the Gateway token from the E2E state dir to manage a dynamic subscription without plugin restart.
- Provider ingress remains standard HTTP webhook POST; WebSocket is used only for OpenClaw control-plane management.

Meego/Meegle status:

- Not completed. I did not mark Meego as passed.
- Correct CLI package identified: `@lark-project/meegle` (`meegle` bin, version `1.0.5`). It exposes work item CRUD/query/comment commands and supports device-code auth.
- Current Meegle auth state is not complete: `auth status` returns `authenticated: false` for `meego.larkoffice.com`; device-code polling currently returns `authorization_pending`.
- `@lark-project/meegle inspect --refresh` lists workitem/comment/project/view/workflow/etc. commands but no webhook/event/subscribe/callback management command.
- `lark-cli event list --json` exposes IM events but no Meego work-item-created event.
- `lark-cli schema project`, `lark-cli schema meego`, and `lark-cli schema workitem` all return unknown service; available registered services do not include Meego/Project.
- Local `.meegle/cache/tools.json` contains work item CRUD/comment/query tools (`create_workitem`, `add_comment`, `list_workitem_comments`, etc.) but no webhook/event/subscribe/callback tool.
- Feishu/Lark OpenAPI llms indexes document the generic event-subscription mechanisms but did not expose a Meego/work-item-created event or webhook subscription API in the available docs indexes.
- Completing Meego requires successful Meegle authorization plus either a real Meego project/admin webhook configuration surface or an official project API that can point `requirement.created` events at `/plugins/webhooks/meego-requirement-created`.
